definition: A sequence of data processing steps
example: The ETL pipeline processes the data nightly.

definition: A structured collection of data
example: The dataset contains 10,000 rows.

definition: A representation of data used for predictions
example: We trained a linear regression model.

definition: How well a system or model works
example: Model performance improved after tuning.

definition: The percentage of correct predictions
example: The model reached 95% accuracy.

definition: Time delay in a system
example: The API has a latency of 200ms.

definition: Able to handle growing amounts of work
example: We built a scalable microservice architecture.

definition: Improve performance or efficiency
example: We optimized the query to run faster.

definition: A step-by-step procedure for calculations or problem-solving
example: We implemented a sorting algorithm to organize the data.

definition: Application Programming Interface, a way for software to communicate
example: The frontend fetches data from the backend via an API.

definition: A lightweight, portable, self-sufficient environment for running applications
example: We deployed the app using Docker containers.

definition: A platform or structure for developing software
example: We built the project using the Django framework.

definition: Time delay experienced in a system
example: The network latency was causing slow responses.

definition: A small, independent service that performs a specific task
example: Each microservice can be deployed separately.

definition: To restructure code without changing its functionality
example: We need to refactor this module to improve readability.

definition: A storage location for code and version control
example: The code is hosted on a GitHub repository.

definition: Ability of a system to handle growth efficiently
example: The application needs scalability for millions of users.

definition: The smallest sequence of programmed instructions that can be executed
example: The program uses multiple threads to improve performance.

definition: The amount of data processed by a system in a given time
example: The server’s throughput increased after the optimization.

definition: Creating a virtual version of something, like hardware or OS
example: We used virtualization to run multiple OS instances on one machine.

definition: A request for information from a database
example: We wrote an SQL query to fetch user data.

definition: A set of data processing steps chained together
example: The ETL pipeline transforms raw data into a clean dataset.

definition: A database structure that improves query performance
example: We added an index to speed up searches on the users table.
